topic: Agentic AI for reliable academic literature review and hallucination mitigation in large language models
final_review:
The integration of Agentic AI in academic literature review has the potential to enhance the reliability and efficiency of the process (Kasneci et al., 2023). However, one of the significant challenges associated with large language models is the need for transparent and accountable AI practices (Barredo Arrieta et al., 2019). Explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) can be employed to provide insights into the decision-making process of the model, helping to identify potential biases and errors (Barredo Arrieta et al., 2019).

The use of XAI can promote responsible AI practices, which is essential for maintaining the integrity of academic research (Barredo Arrieta et al., 2019). Furthermore, the application of Agentic AI in academic literature review can facilitate the analysis of large volumes of data, enabling researchers to identify patterns and trends that may not be apparent through traditional methods (Zawacki-Richter et al., 2019). It is crucial to ensure that the AI models used are transparent, accountable, and free from biases (Dwivedi et al., 2019).

The importance of responsible AI practices in academic research cannot be overstated, as it directly impacts the validity and reliability of the findings (Dwivedi et al., 2019). By promoting transparency and accountability in AI models, researchers can enhance the credibility of their research and maintain the integrity of the academic community. The integration of Agentic AI in academic literature review can be a valuable tool in this endeavor, as long as it is accompanied by rigorous evaluation and validation procedures (Kasneci et al., 2023).

In conclusion, the integration of Agentic AI in academic literature review has the potential to enhance the reliability and efficiency of the process. However, it is essential to address the challenge of ensuring transparent and accountable AI practices through the use of XAI and other mitigation strategies. By promoting responsible AI practices, researchers can ensure the integrity and validity of their findings (Dwivedi et al., 2023).

References:
Barredo Arrieta, A., Díaz-Rodríguez, N., Del Ser, J. (2019)
Dwivedi, Y. K., Hughes, L., Ismagilova, E. (2019)
Dwivedi, Y. K., Kshetri, N., Hughes, L. (2023)
Kasneci, E., Seßler, K., Küchemann, S. (2023)
Zawacki-Richter, O., Marín, V. I., Bond, M. (2019)
